title: Sweet Dense Ethiopian Lentil Wrap
description: A comforting, low‑calorie wrap packed with sweet‑spiced lentils, fresh veggies, and a corn tortilla hug.
instructions: Rinse red lentils until water runs clear.

In a saucepan combine lentils, water, Shitima spice mix, and 1/4 tsp salt; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender and mixture thickens.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté until onion is translucent (~5 minutes).
Add grated carrot and tomato paste to the skillet, cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the cooked lentils, honey, and vegetable broth. Reduce heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes, letting flavors meld and the sauce thicken.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave for 15–20 seconds until pliable.
Spoon 3–4 tablespoons of lentil filling onto the center of each tortilla.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the filling.
Fold the sides of the tortilla over the filling and roll tightly to form a wrap. If desired, press the wrap lightly on a skillet to seal the edges.
For meal prep, cool wraps completely, wrap individually in parchment or foil, and refrigerate up to 4 days. Reheat in a toaster oven or skillet for 2–3 minutes until warmed.
